metadata.title: Why "Allow?" Is Not an Approval Gate
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of claude-agentic-ai candidate 05. Six missing pieces of Allow? only — gate presence vs gate function, what information is needed for supervision to work.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL #1F6F5C = functioning gate / complete information / real decision; CRIMSON #BF3339 = missing information / rubber stamp / no oversight; never swap mid-film
metadata.note: Card exclusions honored: NO four-response gate taxonomy in depth; NO risk-reversibility 2x2 grid explanation; NO James Reason Swiss cheese model.

beats.narration_text: A researcher clicks Allow. She has clicked it forty times in two days. Each task completed fine. She does not know what a single command did.
beats.scene_description: a researcher at a computer, dialog box visible — editorial collage, newsprint texture, no faces
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] researcher at computer with approval dialog

beats.narration_text: This is a gate. It is not functioning as one. A gate that gets clicked without being read is not supervision. It is the performance of supervision.
beats.scene_description: sparse dialog box with only 'Allow?' — CRIMSON outline — counter showing 40 clicks

beats.narration_text: A human approval dialog should prevent unauthorized or mistaken actions. Here is the case where forty approvals provided zero meaningful oversight. Why?
beats.scene_description: question card — CARD copy: Why did forty approvals provide zero oversight?

beats.narration_text: A gate that only says Allow? is missing six things. What action the agent wants to take. What the target is. Why the agent thinks this action is needed. What the risk is. Whether it is reversible. And what to check afterward.
beats.scene_description: dialog box fills in: six labeled fields appear one by one — Action, Target, Reason, Risk, Reversible, Check afterward — each TEAL as it fills in
beats.new_visual_element: [MANIM] gate dialog filling with six fields

beats.narration_text: Without those six, the human is clicking a button, not making a decision.
beats.scene_description: section card — CARD copy: Clicking Allow is not the same as deciding.

beats.narration_text: On approval thirty-one, the agent deletes six functions flagged as unused. Three of them power a production configuration the agent never read. The approval dialog looked identical to the thirty before it.
beats.scene_description: approval counter 31 — identical sparse dialog — six functions marked CRIMSON deleted — production config connection shown broken

beats.narration_text: Before approving: read the action and the target. If you cannot state the risk, do not approve. The gate exists to make a decision, not to record that you were present.
beats.scene_description: endcard — CARD copy: Read the action. Name the risk. Then decide. / sub: AGENTIC AI
